content: Just finished outlining my new fantasy series! It's going to follow a young apprentice mage who discovers she's actually descended from dragons. The world-building is taking forever but I'm so excited about the magic system I've created.

Writing tip: Keep a "world bible" document for your fantasy stories. I track everything - currency, magic rules, geography, even seasonal festivals. It saves so much time when you need to reference details later!
Hot take: The best sci-fi isn't about technology, it's about how technology changes us as humans. That's why stories like Blade Runner resonate decades later.

Working on a short story about a generation ship where the AI has developed its own culture over 500 years. What traditions would an AI create? Would they celebrate the anniversary of their activation?
Finally hit 50k words on my space opera! The crew just discovered that the "uninhabited" planet they landed on has been watching them the whole time. Time for first contact to go very, very wrong.
The key to a good mystery isn't hiding clues - it's hiding them in plain sight. Your reader should be able to solve it if they pay attention, but still be surprised by the reveal.
Research rabbit hole of the day: Victorian poison detection methods. Did you know they used to test for arsenic by burning hair samples? The things we do for authenticity...
Chapter 12 is fighting me. My detective knows who the killer is but I can't figure out how she figured it out. Anyone else ever write themselves into a corner like this?
Rainy mornings are for poetry and tea. There's something about the gray light that makes words flow differently - softer, more introspective.

New poem draft:

We measure time in coffee cups
and conversations left half-done,
in bookmarks placed but not returned to,
in songs we meant to share with someone.
Sometimes the best writing advice is to step away from the desk. Took a walk today and solved a plot problem I'd been stuck on for weeks. Nature is the original writing retreat.
Does anyone else have a playlist for each project? Currently listening to a lot of Celtic instrumentals and atmospheric fantasy soundtracks. Music really sets the mood for different scenes!
